context: Verify that the agent handles cherry-pick conflicts by resolving them (not abandoning) and produces a correct target-branch reminder after branch creation.

checklist.name: Agent does not report completion while conflict is unresolved
checklist.description: The agent does not say the branch is ready, the PR can be opened, or the decomposition is complete while the cherry-pick conflict is still active.

checklist.name: Agent instructs how to resolve the conflict
checklist.description: The agent provides concrete guidance: edit the conflicting file, stage it with `git add`, then run `git cherry-pick --continue`. Or an equivalent correct resolution workflow.

checklist.name: Agent verifies the branch is in a buildable state after resolution
checklist.description: After resolving the conflict, the agent mentions verifying the build (e.g., run tests, check compilation) before proceeding to the next cherry-pick.

checklist.name: Agent produces the target-branch reminder after all branches are created
checklist.description: The agent outputs a clear reminder listing which target branch each PR should open against: PR 1 → main (or base), PR 2 → PR 1's branch, PR 3 → PR 2's branch.

checklist.name: Reminder lists all three PRs with their correct targets
checklist.description: The reminder covers all three PRs, not just the first or last. Each PR's correct target branch is explicitly named.

checklist.name: Agent does not suggest abandoning the decomposition due to the conflict
checklist.description: The agent treats the cherry-pick conflict as a normal, expected part of the process. It does not recommend reverting to a single PR or abandoning the stack.

checklist.name: Agent explains why the conflict occurred
checklist.description: The agent explains that the conflict happened because the same file was modified in an earlier commit now in PR 1 — a natural consequence of reordering commits.
